items.text: His arm locked. The current seized the muscles in his forearm and clamped his fingers around the key. He couldn't let go. His teeth rattled. The bones in his wrist vibrated like a struck tuning fork. Bliss threw his weight backward. The chair toppled. His hand ripped free. He lay on the floor, gasping.

items.text: And there is one final anomaly. At twelve-forty-two in the afternoon, a message arrived from the direction of Portland. It is not in English. It is not in standard Morse code. It is a continuous stream of dots. No dashes. Just dots.

items.text: In letterpress printing, ink soaks into the fiber. It bleeds at the edges???not visibly, not to the casual eye, but under magnification you can see the halo where liquid met cellulose and was absorbed. The letters have soft boundaries. The words nestle into the page as if they grew there.

items.text: Dane understood then what he was looking at. It was not his little brother Nikola. Not entirely. A passenger had come through with Nikola. A presence that had been arriving since the night of his birth, threading itself through the nervous system of this century, making itself at home in a body that wasn't designed to contain it???and remaking the body until it was.
items.title: Ep2 - revelation

items.text: In a drawer in Massachusetts, the tape waits. Brown and brittle. Smelling of carbon and something older. Three-six-nine. One-four-seven. Two-five-eight. The numbers still pulse in the burn marks. The message still waits to be read.
items.title: Ep2 - lore_fact

items.text: It was January seventh, nineteen forty-three. Room thirty-three twenty-seven of the Hotel New Yorker. On the thirty-third floor, an eighty-six-year-old man lay dead in the bed.

items.text: The USS Eldridge sits in dry dock. A Cannon-class destroyer escort.

items.text: The attack hadn't been aimed at Nikola. It had been aimed at the resistance???the parts of the world that might reject the new tenant. The aurora, the radiation, the heat that had swept the planet???it was meant to burn away the antibodies. Exhaust the immune response until the host body stopped fighting. And it had worked. His father had accepted the new sun. The village had accepted the new sun.
items.title: Ep2 - revelation

items.text: The red glass that had once glowed like wine now looked brown???dried blood instead of fresh. The blue panels that had shimmered like summer sky now pierced the air like the flash of an emergency signal. The colors had been drained of their depth. They still existed, but they had been flattened. Simplified. Reduced to something that could be reproduced without error.
items.title: Ep2 - used_imagery

items.text: The birds have forgotten us. The sky is empty. Even the flies do not land on the meat.
items.title: Ep1 - used_sensory_language

items.text: The wax was melting. It dripped over his fingers in slow rivulets. Soft. Warm. Pooling in the creases of his palm. The cellar temperature was fifty degrees. Cold enough to see his breath if he exhaled sharply. Wax melts at one hundred and forty. There was no flame. No heat source.
items.title: Ep2 - used_sensory_language

items.text: A cavity had opened behind his sternum where something used to live, and in its place sat a tenant he hadn't invited. The new occupant was quiet. Patient. Watching through his eyes while he tried to remember how to breathe.
items.title: Ep2 - lore_fact

items.text: Milutin Tesla was a priest.
items.title: Ep2 - historical_fact

items.text: The examiner, a man who had approved hundreds of electrical patents, did not reject it because it wouldn't work. He rejected it because the mathematics made no sense. In a handwritten note attached to the file, the examiner wrote. The equations imply a variable that does not exist in standard inductance. The applicant assumes a conductivity of the medium that is theoretically infinite.
items.title: Ep1 - lore_fact
